skill.name: quill
skill.description: 文风蒸馏器 - 从文章或作家名中蒸馏写作风格，生成可执行的专属Skill。支持单一蒸馏、风格融合、去AI味审计、叙事结构控制、风格强度调节。
skill.summary: A writing style distillation engine that extracts 6-dimensional style profiles from text or author names, then applies them to rewrite content with adjustable intensity, style blending, and anti-AI-pattern auditing.
skill.author: sevenwoood

security_audit.summary: Quill is a prompt-based writing style distillation skill containing no executable code. The 173 'external_commands' findings are false positives — the static scanner matched markdown code fence delimiters (triple backticks) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. The 2 'network' findings reference standard GitHub clone URLs in the README. The 12 'filesystem' findings refer to standard skill installation paths (~/.claude/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/). The obfuscation/entropy warnings reflect naturally high entropy in mixed Chinese-English prose documentation. No malicious intent or code execution vectors were found. The skill is a text-processing prompt template with explicit ethical guidelines against plagiarism.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner detected 173 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns. On inspection, these are all standard markdown code fence delimiters (triple backticks) used to format output examples and code blocks in documentation. This is a false positive — the skill contains no executable code and no shell command injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub clone URL in installation instructions
low_findings.description: The README contains a hardcoded GitHub URL (https://github.com/sevenwoood/quill) for manual installation. This is a standard documentation pattern for open-source skills and does not represent a data exfiltration or network attack vector. Users voluntarily execute the clone command.

low_findings.title: Installation path references to home directory
low_findings.description: The README documents standard skill installation directories: ~/.claude/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/, ~/.codex/skills/. The static scanner flagged these as 'hidden file access' patterns. These are legitimate, publicly documented installation paths for AI coding tool skills.

low_findings.title: High file entropy flagged as potential obfuscation
low_findings.description: Multiple files (SKILL.md, README.md, examples/*.md, references/*.md) were flagged for high entropy (6.2-7.5 bits). On inspection, this is naturally high entropy from mixed Chinese-English bilingual prose with markdown formatting, tables, and emoji. No encrypted or binary content is present. The .DS_Store long-line findings are macOS metadata files, not skill content.

content.user_title: Distill and replicate any writing style with AI
content.value_statement: AI-generated text often sounds formulaic and lacks a distinctive voice. Quill solves this by distilling writing styles from articles or author names into reusable style profiles, enabling natural, human-like text generation with adjustable intensity and style blending.
actual_capabilities: Distills writing styles from text samples or author names into 6-dimensional style profiles (vocabulary, syntax, rhetoric, psychological distance, rhythm, and narrative timeline)
Generates reusable style skill cards that can be applied to rewrite any text in the target style
Blends multiple author styles with customizable per-dimension ratios (e.g., 70% Author A + 30% Author B)
Audits output for AI-pattern indicators and applies anti-AI rewriting rules to produce more natural text
Offers three intensity levels: light polish (30%), full rewrite (60%), and deep reimagining (90%)
Supports incremental skill updates by adding new sample texts and detecting style drift
limitations: Style fidelity depends on the quantity and quality of input samples — fewer than 3 short articles may produce unreliable profiles
Cannot replicate the creative intent or emotional depth of original authors — it captures surface-level patterns only
Style blending works best with 2-3 authors; combining many styles can produce incoherent results
Works as a prompt template only — no code execution, file manipulation, or API integration is included

use_cases.title: Content writer seeking a distinctive voice
use_cases.description: A blogger or content marketer who wants their articles to have a consistent, recognizable style across all posts. They can feed their best 3-5 articles into Quill to generate a personal style profile, then apply it to all future drafts.

use_cases.title: Author studying literary techniques
use_cases.description: A fiction writer learning craft by analyzing how master authors construct sentences, use imagery, and control pacing. They can input an author's name, get a detailed style breakdown, and use it as a reference for their own writing practice.

use_cases.title: Marketing team unifying brand voice
use_cases.description: A team producing many pieces of content (social media, newsletters, web copy) that needs a consistent tone. They can distill their best-performing content into a style profile and apply it to ensure brand voice consistency across all writers.

prompt_templates.title: Distill style from an author name
prompt_templates.prompt: Distill the writing style of [author name]. Analyze 3-5 representative works and generate a complete style skill card covering vocabulary, syntax, rhetoric, psychological distance, rhythm, and narrative timeline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start with a well-known author

prompt_templates.title: Distill style from your own writing samples
prompt_templates.prompt: I will paste 3-5 of my own short articles. Please analyze them for style consistency, detect any style drift across periods, and generate a personalized writing style skill card based on the dominant patterns you find.

prompt_templates.title: Blend two author styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Blend the writing styles of [Author A] and [Author B]. First distill each independently, then create a blended style profile where vocabulary is 70% A and 30% B, syntax is 50/50, and rhetoric follows A's patterns. Generate the blended skill card.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a style to rewrite existing content
prompt_templates.prompt:
Apply the [style name] skill card to rewrite the following text at [light/medium/deep] intensity. After rewriting, provide a modification note showing what changed in vocabulary, syntax, rhetoric, and psychological distance.

Text to rewrite: [paste your text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Rewriting content in a target style

output_examples.input: Apply the 'Yu Hua' style to rewrite: 'The sun was shining brightly. I walked down the street and saw a stray dog. It looked hungry and sad. I felt sorry for it.'
output_examples.output: The street was scorching. A dog lay in the shade of a wall — ribs showing through its fur like piano keys. It looked up at me with eyes that had already given up. I stopped walking. My chest tightened the way it does when you see something that cannot be fixed.

output_examples.input: Distill the style of Eileen Chang (Zhang Ailing) from 3 sample texts
output_examples.output: Zhang Ailing Writing Engine V1.0 — Core Directive: Observe the mundane through layers of irony and sensory precision. Vocabulary: Dense with color and texture words (crimson, jade, velvet). Syntax: Long, winding sentences with frequent parenthetical asides. Rhetoric: Metaphor-heavy, often comparing human emotions to objects (loneliness as a cold perfume). Psychological distance: Close third-person, intimate but detached. Rhythm: Slow, luxurious pacing with sudden short declarative punches.

best_practices: Feed 3-5 high-quality sample articles of 1000+ words each for the most accurate style distillation
Start with medium intensity (60%) when applying a new style — it balances fidelity to the target style with preservation of your original meaning
Use the incremental update feature to refine your style profile over time as you collect more representative writing samples
anti_patterns: Do not use Quill to produce content intended to deceive readers into believing it was written by a specific real author — this violates the skill's ethical guidelines
Do not expect reliable results from fewer than 3 sample texts or from very short inputs (under 500 words total)
Do not blend more than 3 distinct styles at once — the resulting profile will be incoherent and produce low-quality output

faq.question: What AI tools does Quill work with?
faq.answer: Quill is a prompt-based skill that works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It requires no code execution or API integration — it operates entirely through natural language instructions.

faq.question: How many sample texts do I need to distill a style?
faq.answer: For best results, provide 3-5 articles of at least 1000 words each. For well-known authors, you can also input just the author name and Quill will use its internal knowledge to generate a style profile.

faq.question: Can I blend multiple author styles?
faq.answer: Yes. Quill supports blending up to 3 author styles with customizable per-dimension ratios. You can specify different blend ratios for vocabulary, syntax, rhetoric, and other dimensions independently.

faq.question: Is it ethical to use Quill to write like a specific author?
faq.answer: Quill includes built-in ethical guidelines that prohibit word-for-word copying, false attribution (e.g., presenting AI output as a real author's work), and harmful content. It is designed for style learning and creative inspiration, not deception.

faq.question: What is the difference between light, medium, and deep intensity?
faq.answer: Light (30%) preserves your original structure and only replaces vocabulary and minor phrasing. Medium (60%) fully rewrites the text in the target style while keeping the core meaning. Deep (90%) uses your text only as a seed idea and produces a fully reimagined piece.

faq.question: Does Quill execute any code or access my files?
faq.answer: No. Quill is a pure prompt template. It does not execute code, make network requests, or access your filesystem. All processing happens through the AI conversation.
